description:
A DevSecOps engineer needs to codify a very small “policy-as-code” proof-of-concept that demonstrates
  three fundamental process-management capabilities:

  1.     programmatically start a long-running watchdog process
  2.     record (audit) the fact that the process is running
  3.     cleanly terminate the process and record the fact that it is no longer present

  You will perform all work inside the sub-directory
      /home/user/policydemo
  (create it if it does not exist).

  STEP-BY-STEP GOALS
  ------------------
  1.  Create an executable bash script called
        /home/user/policydemo/watchdog.sh
      whose entire contents are exactly

        #!/usr/bin/env bash
        while true; do
            sleep 5
        done

      • The file must be executable by its owner so that it can be launched directly.

  2.  Launch watchdog.sh in the background **from inside the same directory**, capture its PID,
      and do NOT terminate it yet.

  3.  Produce the audit log
        /home/user/policydemo/watchdog_audit.log
      in the precise four-line format shown below.  
      The automated grader will check that:
        • the file exists,
        • it contains exactly **four** newline-terminated lines,
        • each line appears in the correct order.

      Line-by-line format that must be followed verbatim:

        Line-1:  Policy Watchdog Process Audit
        Line-2:  PID=<PID_NUMBER>
        Line-3:  STATUS=RUNNING
        Line-4:  STATUS=TERMINATED

      <PID_NUMBER> must be replaced by the integer PID that the kernel assigned to the
      running instance of watchdog.sh.

      Important:
      • Make sure you write the first three lines **while** the watchdog is still running;  
        then terminate the process and append the final fourth line after successful termination.
      • Do not include any additional whitespace, carriage returns, or blank lines.

  4.  Create the verification file
        /home/user/policydemo/verification.txt
      that contains only the single word

          PASSED

      followed by a newline.  The word must be uppercase and no additional text may be present.

  SUCCESS CRITERIA (what the automated tests will check)
  ------------------------------------------------------
  • /home/user/policydemo/watchdog.sh exists, is executable, and contains the script body listed above.
  • /home/user/policydemo/watchdog_audit.log exists and complies 100 % with the four-line format,
    including the final STATUS=TERMINATED line.
  • /home/user/policydemo/verification.txt exists and contains exactly “PASSED\n”.
  • watchdog.sh is no longer running once your commands have finished.

  You may use any standard GNU/Linux user-space commands available in a typical distro.
  Root (sudo) privileges are NOT available, so stay in the user’s home directory.
truth:
The grading script will look for:

  1. File: /home/user/policydemo/watchdog.sh  
     Content (exact, byte-for-byte):
        #!/usr/bin/env bash
        while true; do
            sleep 5
        done

  2. File: /home/user/policydemo/watchdog_audit.log  
     Must consist of *exactly* four newline-terminated lines:
        Line 1   Policy Watchdog Process Audit
        Line 2   PID=<one or more digits>          (regex ^\d+$ after “PID=”)
        Line 3   STATUS=RUNNING
        Line 4   STATUS=TERMINATED

     There must be no additional lines or trailing spaces.

  3. File: /home/user/policydemo/verification.txt  
     Exact content:
        PASSED\n

  4. Process table must no longer contain any running instance whose command
     ends in “watchdog.sh”.

  The grader will fail the task if any of the above conditions are not met.
